stories.headline: Drilling for lease deals
stories.summary: An investigation into oil and gas leasing on federal lands, highlighting the major players and examining if industry rules are followed.

stories.headline: Diving deep into marine park problems
stories.summary: A look into issues faced by marine mammals in captivity at U.S. marine parks, revealing deaths and inadequate care.

stories.headline: Data helps probe death on the tracks
stories.summary: An examination of rail-crossing safety following a fatal train accident, using data to reveal maintenance lapses.

stories.headline: Reservation deaths uncovered with data
stories.summary: An investigation into the high death rates of children at the Warm Springs Reservation in Oregon, using state databases.

stories.headline: Changes for the Database Library
stories.author_title: NICAR and Missouri School of Journalism
stories.summary: The IRE and NICAR Database Library introduces its new staff members, including several master's students and visiting journalists specializing in investigative reporting, data analysis, and digital journalism. The organization also highlights recent departures of staff members who have moved on to positions at major news organizations.

stories.headline: Scrape away
stories.summary: IRE and NICAR have launched the Scraping Project to help journalists efficiently extract data from online public databases by sharing and collaborating on web scraping scripts that can be modified and reused.

stories.headline: Extra! Extra!
stories.summary: Extra! Extra! is an online resource from IRE that catalogs investigative and computer-assisted reporting stories, allowing journalists to search by category and discover relevant reporting techniques for their beats.

stories.headline: Debunking some myths about speeders
stories.summary: A journalist describes their first computer-assisted reporting project analyzing 1.6 million municipal court records from Bexar County, Texas, focusing on data import procedures, database management in Microsoft Access, and isolating speeding violations from 400,000+ records.

stories.headline: Unmasking murder myths
stories.summary: A Pittsburgh journalist investigates the city's record homicide rate in 2003, discovering that murders are concentrated in specific neighborhoods and disproportionately involve young Black victims, and uses data mapping to present these findings clearly without reinforcing stereotypes.

stories.headline: NICAR has transportation data for journalists
stories.summary: The IRE and NICAR Database Library provides access to updated U.S. Department of Transportation databases that journalists can use to investigate safety issues in the trucking industry and identify dangerous roads.

stories.headline: Automate common tasks with FoxPro programs
stories.summary: The IRE and NICAR Database Library automated routine tasks like updating Federal Election Commission and FAA data, and developed a system to automate state-sliced data extraction from large databases, learning important lessons about handling inconsistent data structures across different government agencies.

stories.headline: Building a no-cost, open newsroom
stories.summary: This article introduces a series on implementing free, open-source software tools in newsrooms to create a computer-assisted reporting platform, starting with MySQL as a database server and outlining basic hardware requirements and future topics.
